title: PutObject, Metadata, Checksums, and Encryption Defaults · Execution Trace: Write Result Evidence
summary: A service uploads an unbounded reader, uses the returned ETag as a universal digest, loses content metadata on replacement, and cannot prove which bytes S3 accepted. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the write result evidence increment in single-object upload and integrity evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Record bucket, key, version, checksum, size, request ID, encryption, policy decision, and changed-read verification without logging secrets.
Verify S3 default encryption and choose SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS requirements without claiming transport or client-side confidentiality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Write Result Evidence · Theory Write Result Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 6602 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove accepted bytes, checksum, metadata, and encryption without using ETag as a universal digest; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record bucket, key, version, checksum, size, request ID, encryption, policy decision, and changed-read verification without logging secrets.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Write Result Evidence contract for the supplied data.
